req.title: Read patient (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three distinct verification axes: (1) a clinician assigned to a patient receives the resource and an AuditEvent is written (EP positive path), (2) a clinician NOT assigned is denied with HTTP 403 and an AuditEvent is still written (NEG/DT error path), and (3) a boundary condition where a clinician is assigned to exactly one patient and reads that sole patient – the minimal valid assignment set (BVA). A fourth State-Transition test covers the assignment lifecycle: clinician has no assignment, is assigned, reads successfully, is unassigned, reads and is denied – exercising the state machine implied by 'patients in their care'. Together these four tests satisfy the minimum-three requirement while providing comprehensive requirement coverage traceable to R-HLT-077.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_and_audit_written
preconditions: Patient resource exists and is assigned to the clinician
actions: Authenticate clinician and obtain bearer token
Query AuditEvent store for an event referencing this patient read
expected: Response id matches requested patient id
At least one AuditEvent exists for this operation with outcome 0 (success)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_and_audit_written(
    assigned_clinician_token,
    assigned_patient_id,
    fhir_base_url,
    audit_query
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert body["id"] == assigned_patient_id

    events = audit_query(
        entity_id=assigned_patient_id,
        action="R"
    )
    assert len(events) >= 1
    assert events[0]["outcome"] == "0"

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_denied_and_audit_written
preconditions: Patient resource exists but is NOT assigned to the clinician
actions: Authenticate clinician and obtain bearer token
GET /Patient/{other_id} for a patient NOT in the clinician's assignment
Query AuditEvent store for a denial event referencing this patient read
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome or error indicator
At least one AuditEvent exists for this operation with outcome 8 (serious failure / denied)
trace: R-HLT-077: access audit written for denied operations
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unassigned_clinician_denied_and_audit_written(
    unassigned_clinician_token,
    unassigned_patient_id,
    fhir_base_url,
    audit_query
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {unassigned_clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{unassigned_patient_id}",
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "resourceType" in body  # OperationOutcome expected

    events = audit_query(
        entity_id=unassigned_patient_id,
        action="R"
    )
    assert len(events) >= 1
    denied = [e for e in events if e["outcome"] != "0"]
    assert len(denied) >= 1

tests.name: test_clinician_with_single_assignment_reads_that_patient
preconditions: Clinician is assigned to exactly ONE patient (minimum assignment cardinality)
actions: Authenticate clinician and obtain bearer token
GET /Patient/{sole_patient_id} for the single assigned patient
Query AuditEvent store for a success event for this read
expected: Response id matches the sole assigned patient id
AuditEvent with outcome 0 exists for this read
trace: BVA: lower boundary of valid assignment set size = 1
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_clinician_with_single_assignment_reads_that_patient(
    single_assignment_clinician_token,
    sole_assigned_patient_id,
    fhir_base_url,
    audit_query
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {single_assignment_clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{sole_assigned_patient_id}",
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == sole_assigned_patient_id

    events = audit_query(
        entity_id=sole_assigned_patient_id,
        action="R"
    )
    success = [e for e in events if e["outcome"] == "0"]
    assert len(success) >= 1

tests.name: test_assignment_lifecycle_transitions_access_and_audit
actions: State S0: clinician has no assignment – attempt read, expect 403 + audit denial
Transition T1: assign clinician to patient via assignment API
State S1: clinician is assigned – attempt read, expect 200 + audit success
Transition T2: remove clinician assignment via assignment API
State S2: clinician unassigned again – attempt read, expect 403 + audit denial
expected: S0 read returns HTTP 403 and an AuditEvent with non-zero outcome
S1 read returns HTTP 200 with Patient body and AuditEvent outcome 0
S2 read returns HTTP 403 and a new AuditEvent with non-zero outcome
Total AuditEvents for entity >= 3 (one per operation)
trace: R-HLT-077: access audit written for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_assignment_lifecycle_transitions_access_and_audit(
    clinician_token,
    lifecycle_patient_id,
    fhir_base_url,
    assign_patient,
    unassign_patient,
    audit_query
):
    hdrs = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    url = f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{lifecycle_patient_id}"

    r0 = requests.get(url, headers=hdrs)
    assert r0.status_code == 403

    assign_patient(clinician_token, lifecycle_patient_id)

    r1 = requests.get(url, headers=hdrs)
    assert r1.status_code == 200
    assert r1.json()["id"] == lifecycle_patient_id

    unassign_patient(clinician_token, lifecycle_patient_id)

    r2 = requests.get(url, headers=hdrs)
    assert r2.status_code == 403

    events = audit_query(entity_id=lifecycle_patient_id, action="R")
    assert len(events) >= 3
    outcomes = [e["outcome"] for e in events]
    assert "0" in outcomes
    assert any(o != "0" for o in outcomes)
